frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Bro exactly, little makeup is fine but if someone looks totally different without it then obviously the guy has a right to know before getting serious. Matlab what are you hiding if your real face and Insta face are two different people 😂
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's makeup use as deception and suggests men are entitled to access a woman's 'real' appearance before commitment. It encodes casual suspicion, entitlement, and policing of women's self-presentation in a joking WhatsApp tone.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
